title: obus Package — Agent Memory
text: Package: obusLocation: /Users/einarhj/R/Pakkar/obusPurpose: Fast, tidy access ICES DATRAS (trawl survey) data. Eliminates local data maintenance; provides consistent column types, standardized units, unique haul identifiers.

title: obus Package — Agent Memory
code: devtools::load_all("/Users/einarhj/R/Pakkar/obus")

title: obus Package — Agent Memory
text: Git LFS tracks data/*.rda via .gitattributes — usethis::use_data(..., overwrite = TRUE), verify file staged actual content, just pointer.

title: obus Package — Agent Memory
text: surveys = NULL → defaults ICES surveys excluding test surveys (via .dr_default_surveys(), calls icesDatras::getSurveyList() drops anything matching ^Test). aphia = NULL → defaults cod (126436), haddock (126437), herring (126417) record types require species codes ("CW", "IDX").
code: dr_get(recordtype, surveys = NULL, years = 1965:2030, quarters = 1:4,        aphia = NULL, from = "parquet", quiet = TRUE)

title: obus Package — Agent Memory
text: ignored FL, LT, CPUEL, CPUEA, CW, IDX.

title: obus Package — Agent Memory
text: Returns lazy duckdbfs tibble connected “-” ICES parquet files original old-style column names (Ship, HaulNo, ShootLat, etc.). Valid tables: "HH", "HL", "CA", "FL", "LT", "CPUEL", "CPUEA", "CW", "IDX". Use need unmodified ICES output.
code: dr_con_raw(table = "HH")

title: obus Package — Agent Memory
text: Returns lazy duckdbfs tibble tidied parquet data. Types accepted: "HH", "HL", "CA", "species", "haul", "dictionary", "vocabulary", "cpuelength".
code: dr_con(type, trim = TRUE, url = "https://heima.hafro.is/~einarhj/datras", quiet = TRUE)

title: obus Package — Agent Memory
text: return object type input (pipeable). Work data frames DuckDB lazy tables noted.

title: obus Package — Agent Memory
text: Typical usage rename raw (old-style) columns tidy (new-style): Reversibility caveat: 7 new names map one old name; dr_translate(..., = "new", = "old") silently picks first match. Affected names: reliable new→old translation, filter dictionary specific table first.
code: dictionary <- dr_lookup_fields |> select(old, new) |> distinct() dr_con_raw("HH") |> dr_translate(dictionary, from = "old", to = "new")

title: obus Package — Agent Memory
text: Applies column types dr_lookup_fields. Called per data frame bind_rows fetchers. name_col = "new" → new-style names (parquet/new API); default name_col = "old" → old-style names (getDATRAS, FL, LT, CW, CPUEL, CPUEA, IDX) recordheader: filter specific table value, NULL use Replaces literal "NA" strings real NA coercion -9 sentinel values replaced NA fetchers ()

title: obus Package — Agent Memory
text: Columns: table (record type), new (new-style column name get_datras_unaggregated_data), old (old-style column name getDATRAS derived products), DataFormat (“char”/“int”/“decimal”), Description. new column filled derived tables (CPUEL, CPUEA, IDX) matching old names HH/HL/CA source mapping. Columns HH/HL/CA counterpart (e.g. AphiaID, Species, ShootLon, LngtClas, DateTime, CPUE_number_per_hour) remain NA new. regenerate editing type specs ICES field list changes: script fetches ICES web service, applies case_when fixes known type ambiguities, appends hand-curated entries FL, LT, CPUEL, CPUEA, IDX, fills missing new values HH/HL/CA source mapping. Type priority rule: char > decimal > int. Key type fixes applied case_when (using old names): - HaulNumber → int - Distance → decimal - StationName → char - CANoAtLngt → decimal (consistent HLNoAtLngt)
code: source("data-raw/DATASET_lookup_fields.R")

title: obus Package — Agent Memory
text: LT field name mismatch: ICES web service returns LT entries new-style old values (e.g. Platform, HaulNumber) match actual column names getLTassessment() (e.g. Ship, HaulNo). entries don’t fire .dr_settypes(). Flagged code comments. CPUEA / IDX / LT xsi:nil artifact: Columns data arrive names like `Age_6 xsi:nil="true"` `Tickler xsi:nil="true"`. fetcher strips suffix column names per df bind_rows. CPUEA/IDX, Age_* also coerced numeric step. IDX PlusGr → PlusGrAge: PlusGr column IDX output renamed PlusGrAge .dr_fetch_indices() avoid conflict CA’s PlusGr char flag (“+”). Sex derived tables: CPUEL, CPUEA, IDX return Sex column (old-style name). fill-maps IndividualSex (CA) CA’s entry encountered first — HL mapping give SpeciesSex. correct new-style name Sex derived products unresolved; remains known ambiguity. CW NAs: CatchWgt = NA means species absent haul (missing data). ~40% NA typical two-species query. CPUEL slow: ~30s per survey/year/quarter API call. IDX returns rows silently: species indices defined given survey/quarter (e.g. herring NS-IBTS Q1), tryCatch drops result silently. Expected behaviour. Git LFS: data/*.rda tracked LFS. usethis::use_data(), verify file staged actual content, just pointer.

title: obus Package — Agent Memory
code: library(obus)  # Full parquet download hh <- dr_get("HH") hl <- dr_get("HL")  # Prepare HH hh <- hh |> dr_add_id() |> dr_add_date() |> dr_add_starttime()  # Prepare HL hl <- hl |>   dr_add_id() |>   dr_add_length_cm() |>   dr_add_n_and_cpue() |>   dr_add_record_type()  # Specific survey, recent years, via new API hh_ns <- dr_get("HH", surveys = "NS-IBTS", years = 2020:2024, from = "new")  # Flex file fl <- dr_get("FL", surveys = "NS-IBTS", years = 2020:2023, quarters = 1)  # CPUE by length cpuel <- dr_get("CPUEL", surveys = "NS-IBTS", years = 2018, quarters = 1)  # CPUE by age cpuea <- dr_get("CPUEA", surveys = "NS-IBTS", years = 2018, quarters = 1)  # Catch weight (defaults to cod/haddock/herring if aphia omitted) cw <- dr_get("CW", surveys = "NS-IBTS", years = 2018, quarters = 1,              aphia = c(126436, 126437))  # cod, haddock  # Survey indices (defaults to cod/haddock/herring if aphia omitted) idx <- dr_get("IDX", surveys = "NS-IBTS", years = 2018, quarters = 1,               aphia = c(126436, 126437))  # Raw ICES tables (old-style column names) dr_con_raw("HH") |> dplyr::filter(Year == 2020) |> dplyr::collect()  # Translate raw old-style names to new-style dictionary <- dr_lookup_fields |> dplyr::select(old, new) |> dplyr::distinct() dr_con_raw("HH") |> dr_translate(dictionary, from = "old", to = "new")  # Lazy DuckDB query on tidy parquet dr_con("HL") |>   dplyr::filter(Survey == "NS-IBTS", Year >= 2020) |>   dplyr::collect()

title: Authors and Citation
text: Einar Hjorleifsson. Author, maintainer. Martin Pastoors. Author. Vaishav Soni. Author.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Hjorleifsson E, Pastoors M, Soni V (2026). obus: Implementing Better Use ICES Survey data. R package version 2026.01.30, https://einarhjorleifsson.github.io/obus/.

what: Implementing More and Better Use of ICES Survey data
title: Implementing More and Better Use of ICES Survey data
text: aim {obus} provide users tidy DATRAS tables non-ambiguous variables. said, {obus} temporary experimental package used explore various DATRAS data connections wrapper functions make life little easier everyday user. may taken official package. possibly . far {obus} actually little. purist, one regrets inform package quite number dependencies (see DESCRIPTION). however possible trim fat. information, check README.md.

title: Implementing More and Better Use of ICES Survey data
text: can install development version {obus} GitHub running: cases {obus} uses wrapper functions depending {icesDatras} features , yet, taken official ICES version (issues pending) install package via: two ways connect DATRAS data, either importing whole datasets R making -process DuckDB database connection.
code: remotes::install_github("einarhjorleifsson/obus") remotes::install_github("einarhjorleifsson/icesDatras", force = TRUE) library(obus)

title: Implementing More and Better Use of ICES Survey data
text: fastest way import full DATRAS data R : talking less 5 seconds sitting optic fiber. connected via poor wifi may take closer minute. Whatever case one can assume nobody complain given dimension just imported follows: Number records variables fast access achieved importing parquet files hosted conventional https-server. parquet data source now mirror data residing ICES, recent copy. ICES datacenter currently exploring ways serve mirror DATRAS data via parquet files hosted cloud service. old faithful (icesDatras::getDATRAS) wrapped within function, addition variable type set -9 turned NA. dare call one year HL-data: store now : little faster approach also experimental phase (icesDatras::get_datras_unaggregated_data) yet mirror date data (hence year 2025 used) store now :
code: system.time({   hh <- dr_get("HH", from = "parquet")   hl <- dr_get("HL", from = "parquet")   ca <- dr_get("CA", from = "parquet") }) #>    user  system elapsed  #>   9.615   5.359  31.843 system.time({   hl_old <- dr_get(recordtype = "HL", years = 2026, from = "old", quiet = TRUE) }) #>    user  system elapsed  #>   8.873   4.156  55.653 hl_old |> dplyr::count(Survey) #>     Survey     n #> 1     BITS 25115 #> 2  NS-IBTS 48070 #> 3 SCOWCGFS 12307 system.time({   hl_new <- dr_get("HL", years = 2025, from = "new", quiet = TRUE) }) #>    user  system elapsed  #>   1.085   0.416   9.298 hl_new |> dplyr::count(Survey) #>     Survey     n #> 1     BITS 27084 #> 2      BTS 49393 #> 3     DYFS  4967 #> 4  NL-BSAS  1359 #> 5  NS-IBTS 85341 #> 6   SCOROC  5175 #> 7 SCOWCGFS 10223 #> 8      SNS  2597

title: Implementing More and Better Use of ICES Survey data
text: Although DATRAS data can considered big data, one can use techniques developed datasets. instead importing full dataset R one can generate connection url-hosted parquet files (fully --date data yet) using -process DuckDB database.

title: Implementing More and Better Use of ICES Survey data

"BITS:1991:1:SE:77AR:GOV:71:6", "BITS:1991:1:SE:7… #> $ sur                  <chr> "BITS-1", "BITS-1", "BITS-1", "BITS-1", "BITS-1",… #> $ length_cm            <dbl> 32, 34, 36, 39, 40, 43, 45, 30, 31, 34, 35, 38, 4… #> $ latin                <chr> "Gadus morhua", "Gadus morhua", "Gadus morhua", "… #> $ species              <chr> "Atlantic cod", "Atlantic cod", "Atlantic cod", "…

title: Implementing More and Better Use of ICES Survey data
text: familiar using dplyr-verbs process data function well many base-R functions can used process data. E.g. one can get survey stations 2024 add number cod observed using following script: code steps automatically translated SQL passed -process DuckDB. can view sql via: data yet R, “collect”-step happens: importantly, variables .id (unique station id), sur, lon, lat, latin, n_haul ever passed web. addition certain chunks parquet files (read: rows), chunks “fall within range” filtered values passed web.
code: system.time({   q <-     data <-     # Process the data in DuckDB     dr_con("HH") |>      dplyr::filter(Year == 2024,                   Quarter %in% 1:4) |>      dplyr::select(.id, sur, lon = ShootLongitude, lat = ShootLatitude) |>      dplyr::left_join(dr_con("HL", trim = TRUE) |>                         dplyr::filter(latin == "Gadus morhua") |>                         dplyr::group_by(.id) |>                         dplyr::summarise(n_haul = sum(n_haul, na.rm = TRUE)),                      by = dplyr::join_by(.id)) |>      dplyr::mutate(n_haul = coalesce(n_haul, 0)) }) #>    user  system elapsed  #>   0.093   0.010   0.220 q |> dplyr::glimpse() #> Rows: ?? #> Columns: 5 #> Database: DuckDB 1.5.2 [root@Darwin 25.3.0:R 4.5.2/:memory:] #> $ .id    <chr> "BITS:2024:1:DK:26HF:TVS:104:53", "BITS:2024:1:DK:26HF:TVS:96:4… #> $ sur    <chr> "BITS-1", "BITS-1", "BITS-1", "BITS-1", "BITS-1", "BITS-1", "BI… #> $ lon    <dbl> 10.8619, 10.8261, 10.7020, 15.3746, 12.4116, 10.1898, 13.4157, … #> $ lat    <dbl> 55.5271, 55.3924, 56.9433, 55.3554, 56.1340, 54.7324, 55.1882, … #> $ n_haul <dbl> 12.000, 4.000, 1.000, 321.000, 2937.020, 4.000, 883.050, 461.00… q |> dplyr::show_query() #> <SQL> #> SELECT ".id", sur, lon, lat, COALESCE(n_haul, 0.0) AS n_haul #> FROM ( #>   SELECT LHS.*, n_haul #>   FROM ( #>     SELECT ".id", sur, ShootLongitude AS lon, ShootLatitude AS lat #>     FROM vjipvsndtxylhfz #>     WHERE ("Year" = 2024.0) AND ("Quarter" IN (1, 2, 3, 4)) #>   ) LHS #>   LEFT JOIN ( #>     SELECT ".id", SUM(n_haul) AS n_haul #>     FROM ( #>       SELECT #>         ".id", #>         latin, #>         length_cm, #>         SpeciesSex, #>         DevelopmentStage, #>         n_haul, #>         n_hour #>       FROM cxvkqwwkeuruqcb #>       WHERE (latin = 'Gadus morhua') #>     ) q01 #>     GROUP BY ".id" #>   ) RHS #>     ON (LHS.".id" = RHS.".id") #> ) q01 system.time({   d <- q |> dplyr::collect() }) #>    user  system elapsed  #>   0.139   0.013   0.093 d |> dplyr::glimpse() #> Rows: 4,185 #> Columns: 5 #> $ .id    <chr> "NS-IBTS:2024:3:SE:77SE:GOV:152:5", "NS-IBTS:2024:3:SE:77SE:GOV… #> $ sur    <chr> "NS-IBTS-3", "NS-IBTS-3", "NS-IBTS-3", "NS-IBTS-3", "NS-IBTS-3"… #> $ lon    <dbl> 9.9734, 11.9705, 1.4565, -1.5410, 12.2221, -2.1130, 9.7497, 0.0… #> $ lat    <dbl> 58.0567, 56.3460, 58.2492, 56.7660, 56.8238, 58.1880, 57.6615, … #> $ n_haul <dbl> 13.000, 3.000, 2.000, 6.000, 1.000, 2.000, 2.000, 3.000, 7.000,…

title: Implementing More and Better Use of ICES Survey data
text: stuff development, thus bugs, snags errors expected. {obus} still experimental hangover functions need pruned removed. Actually, now {obus} much, initial focus experimenting fast access DATRAS data. practical purposes one can totally live without . Importing full HH, HL CA data R can achieved (anticipated ICES datacenter maintain official path): duckdb connection can achieved (slightly augmented dataframes, using simple obus wrapper functions):
code: hh <- arrow::read_parquet("https://heima.hafro.is/~einarhj/datras/raw/HH.parquet") hl <- arrow::read_parquet("https://heima.hafro.is/~einarhj/datras/raw/HL.parquet") ca <- arrow::read_parquet("https://heima.hafro.is/~einarhj/datras/raw/CA.parquet") hh <- duckdbfs::open_dataset("https://heima.hafro.is/~einarhj/datras/HH.parquet") hl <- duckdbfs::open_dataset("https://heima.hafro.is/~einarhj/datras/HL.parquet") ca <- duckdbfs::open_dataset("https://heima.hafro.is/~einarhj/datras/CA.parquet")

what: Download and process DATRAS data to parquet files — .dr_download
title: Download and process DATRAS data to parquet files — .dr_download
text: Downloads raw HH, HL, CA records DATRAS API, applies standard tidying transformations, writes results parquet files. Processed data written <path>/. Optionally, unprocessed data can also saved <path>/raw/.

title: Download and process DATRAS data to parquet files — .dr_download
code: .dr_download(path, save_raw = FALSE)

title: Download and process DATRAS data to parquet files — .dr_download
text: path Character. Base directory parquet files written. save_raw Logical. TRUE, saves unprocessed data <path>/raw/ tidying. Default FALSE.

title: Download and process DATRAS data to parquet files — .dr_download
text: Invisibly returns NULL. Called side effects.

title: Row-Wise Value Classifier — .dr_row_classifier
text: function classifies values set input vectors row-wise based specific rules. supports numeric, logical, categorical (non-numeric) vectors, applying type-specific classifications producing combined classification row delimited string.

title: Row-Wise Value Classifier — .dr_row_classifier
code: .dr_row_classifier(...)

title: Row-Wise Value Classifier — .dr_row_classifier
text: ... series numeric, logical, categorical vectors classify. vectors must length.

title: Row-Wise Value Classifier — .dr_row_classifier
text: character vector, one element per row input, containing concatenated classifications row.

title: Row-Wise Value Classifier — .dr_row_classifier
text: Numeric Logical Variables: NA: Missing values classified "NA". 0: Values equal 0 (FALSE logicals) classified "0". >0: Positive values (TRUE logicals) classified ">0". <=0: Negative values classified "<=0". Categorical (Non-Numeric) Variables: "": Non-missing values classified generically "". "NA": Missing values classified "NA".

title: Row-Wise Value Classifier — .dr_row_classifier
text: function outputs combined classification row, concatenated using "_" separate classifications input vectors.

title: Row-Wise Value Classifier — .dr_row_classifier
code: var1 <- c(NA, 0, 1, 2)           # Numeric var2 <- c(0, 1, NA, 3)           # Numeric var3 <- c(NA, NA, 2, 0)          # Numeric var4 <- c("a", "b", NA, "c")     # Categorical var5 <- c(TRUE, FALSE, NA, TRUE) # Logical  classifications <- .dr_row_classifier(var1, var2, var3, var4, var5) print(classifications) #> [1] "NA_0_NA_a_>0"   "0_>0_NA_a_0"    ">0_NA_>0_NA_NA" ">0_>0_0_a_>0"    ## Output: # [1] "NA_0_NA_a_>0" "0_>0_NA_a_0" ">0_NA_>0_NA_NA" ">0_>0_0_a_>0"

what: Calculate date based on Year, Month, and Day. — dr_add_date
title: Calculate date based on Year, Month, and Day. — dr_add_date

title: Calculate date based on Year, Month, and Day. — dr_add_date
code: dr_add_date(d)

title: Calculate date based on Year, Month, and Day. — dr_add_date
text: d DuckDB connection table dataframe containing least columns: Year (numeric integer), Month (numeric integer), Day (numeric integer).

title: Calculate date based on Year, Month, and Day. — dr_add_date
text: input table (DuckDB table dataframe) additional date column, NULL input neither DuckDB table dataframe.

title: Calculate date based on Year, Month, and Day. — dr_add_date
text: function checks required columns Year, Month, Day exist, throws error missing.

what: Generate a unique haul id — dr_add_id
title: Generate a unique haul id — dr_add_id
text: function generates haul ID concatenating fields DATRAS tables. generated ID stored new variable .id. input must contain columns: Survey, Year, Quarter, Country, Platform, Gear, StationName, HaulNumber. columns missing, error raised.

title: Generate a unique haul id — dr_add_id
code: dr_add_id(d, base = "new")

title: Generate a unique haul id — dr_add_id
text: d DATRAS table, one HH, HL, CA. base Vector, either "new" (default) "old"

title: Generate a unique haul id — dr_add_id
text: table (d) additional variable .id

title: Generate a unique haul id — dr_add_id
code: # Example with a simulated DATRAS table example_data <- data.frame(   Survey = "Surv1",   Year = 2026,   Quarter = 1,   Country = "Country1",   Platform = "Platform1",   Gear = "Gear1",   StationName = "StationA",   HaulNumber = 123 ) example_data |> dr_add_id() #>   Survey Year Quarter  Country  Platform  Gear StationName HaulNumber #> 1  Surv1 2026       1 Country1 Platform1 Gear1    StationA        123 #>                                                  .id #> 1 Surv1:2026:1:Country1:Platform1:Gear1:StationA:123

title: Add a standardized length_cm column to the input table — dr_add_length_cm
text: d dataframe DuckDB table containing least two columns: column LengthCode column LengthClass. LengthCode column specifying length code (unquoted). Defaults LengthCode. LengthClass column specifying length class (unquoted). Defaults LengthClass.

what: Numbers caught and the CPUE in each length class — dr_add_n_and_cpue
title: Numbers caught and the CPUE in each length class — dr_add_n_and_cpue
text: function generates two new columns: n (numbers caught) cpue (numbers caught per one hour towing) length table (HL).

title: Numbers caught and the CPUE in each length class — dr_add_n_and_cpue
code: dr_add_n_and_cpue(   d,   NumberAtLength = NumberAtLength,   HaulDuration = HaulDuration,   SubsamplingFactor = SubsamplingFactor )

title: Numbers caught and the CPUE in each length class — dr_add_n_and_cpue
text: d DATRAS length table (HL) containing columns .id, column NumberAtLength, column HaulDuration, column SubsamplingFactor. NumberAtLength column specifying number individuals (unquoted). Defaults NumberAtLength. HaulDuration column specifying haul duration (unquoted). Defaults HaulDuration. SubsamplingFactor column specifying subsampling factor (unquoted). Defaults SubsamplingFactor.

title: Numbers caught and the CPUE in each length class — dr_add_n_and_cpue
text: input table additional columns n_haul n_hour.

what: Classify HL records by measurement type — dr_add_record_type
title: Classify HL records by measurement type — dr_add_record_type
text: Adds record_type integer column HL table classifying row combination variables present absent. See hl_record_type_lookup full definition code.

title: Classify HL records by measurement type — dr_add_record_type
code: dr_add_record_type(d)

title: Classify HL records by measurement type — dr_add_record_type
text: d DATRAS length table (HL) containing least columns .id, ValidAphiaID, LengthClass, n_haul, SpeciesSex, DevelopmentStage, TotalNumber, SpeciesCategoryWeight, SubsamplingFactor.

title: Classify HL records by measurement type — dr_add_record_type
text: d additional integer column record_type.

title: Classify HL records by measurement type — dr_add_record_type
text: function requires dr_add_n_and_cpue already run n_haul present; uses n_haul proxy haul validity (types 1–3 vs type 4) rather reading DataType directly. Classification uses two passes. first pass assigns types row--row using variable presence/absence. second pass requires haul-level context: weight-records (initially type 11) reclassified type 16 development-stage length-frequency record (type 3) exists .id ValidAphiaID, identifying companion weight entries rather standalone bulk bycatch.

what: Calculate timestamp based on Year, Month, Day, and StartTime/TimeShot. — dr_add_starttime
title: Calculate timestamp based on Year, Month, Day, and StartTime/TimeShot. — dr_add_starttime
text: function calculates precise timestamp using Year, Month, Day, StartTime TimeShot columns input table (DuckDB table dataframe). time column expected numeric character string representing time 24-hour HHMM format. ensures required columns present input adjusts behavior based whether input DuckDB table standard dataframe.

title: Calculate timestamp based on Year, Month, Day, and StartTime/TimeShot. — dr_add_starttime
code: dr_add_starttime(d)

title: Calculate timestamp based on Year, Month, Day, and StartTime/TimeShot. — dr_add_starttime
text: d DuckDB connection table dataframe containing least columns: Year (numeric integer), Month (numeric integer), Day (numeric integer), StartTime/TimeShot (numeric character HHMM format).

title: Calculate timestamp based on Year, Month, Day, and StartTime/TimeShot. — dr_add_starttime
text: input table (DuckDB table dataframe) additional timestamp column, calculated POSIXct timestamp, NULL input neither DuckDB table dataframe.

what: Establish a DuckDB Connection to DATRAS Datasets — dr_con
title: Establish a DuckDB Connection to DATRAS Datasets — dr_con
text: function creates DuckDB connection specified DATRAS dataset type, facilitating access trawl survey data stored Parquet format. dataset type determines data loaded remote source.

title: Establish a DuckDB Connection to DATRAS Datasets — dr_con
code: dr_con(   type = NULL,   trim = TRUE,   url = "https://heima.hafro.is/~einarhj/datras",   quiet = TRUE )

title: Establish a DuckDB Connection to DATRAS Datasets — dr_con
text: type character string specifying dataset type. Available values (tables): "HH": Haul-level data. "HL": Catch--length data (filterable via trim option). "CA": Catch--age data (filterable via trim option). "species": Species dataset derived ICES SpecWoRMS. trim Logical. "HL" "CA", TRUE (default), non-essential fields excluded. Ignored datasets. url URL Parquet file directory, currently defaulting "https://heima.hafro./~einarhj/datras". quiet Logical. TRUE (default), suppresses connection warnings messages.

title: Establish a DuckDB Connection to DATRAS Datasets — dr_con
text: DuckDB dataset table.

title: Establish a DuckDB Connection to DATRAS Datasets — dr_con
text: function operates following dataset types: "HH" (Haul-Level Data): Contains information related individual haul events. "HL" (Catch--Length Data): Records catches categorized length class. "CA" (Catch--Age Data): Includes age-based biological data (e.g., liver weight, length). "species" (Species List): Derived ICES vocabulary 'SpecWoRMS' includes species names related metadata.

title: Establish a DuckDB Connection to DATRAS Datasets — dr_con
text: dataset accessed via HTTP/HTTPS paths user-defined default URL location. file names inferred provided type parameter (e.g., Parquet file named "HH.parquet" "HH" type data).

title: Establish a DuckDB Connection to DATRAS Datasets — dr_con
text: dataset types "HH", "HL", "CA", unique identifier column (.id) represent catenation fields Survey, Year, Quarter, Country, Platform, Gear, StationName HaulNumber seperated ":" (see dr_add_id).

title: Establish a DuckDB Connection to DATRAS Datasets — dr_con
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   # Establish connections   dr_con("HH")                   # Connect to haul-level data.   dr_con("HL", trim = FALSE)     # Include all fields for catch-at-length data.   species_data <- dr_con("species")    # Inspect species data   dplyr::glimpse(species_data) } # }

what: Get numbers and weights summary of species by haul — dr_con_by_haul
title: Get numbers and weights summary of species by haul — dr_con_by_haul
text: function calculates summarizes total numbers weights species reported survey. processing fishing haul-level (HH) catch-level (HL) data, generates aggregate number (n_haul) weight (w_haul) species caught per haul, alongside estimates standardized 1-hour haul duration (n_hour, w_hour).

title: Get numbers and weights summary of species by haul — dr_con_by_haul
code: dr_con_by_haul(   latin = "Melanogrammus aeglefinus",   trim = TRUE,   method = "ices" )

title: Get numbers and weights summary of species by haul — dr_con_by_haul
text: latin xxxx trim Boolean (default TRUE), controls additional non-essential variables returned . method xxx

title: Get numbers and weights summary of species by haul — dr_con_by_haul
text: summarized data.frame following columns: .id: Unique haul identifier. latin: Latin species name, identifying species. n_haul: Actual number individuals per haul. w_haul: Actual weight (kg) per haul. n_hour: Number individuals raised 1-hour haul. w_hour: Weight (kg) raised 1-hour haul. trim FALSE additional variables return TotalNumber: Total number individuals species caught - nonstandardized. SpeciesCategoryWeight: Total weight (kg) species caught - nonstandardized. DataType: Type data recording, value "C", SpeciesCategoryWeight unit per 60 minute hauling, otherwise unit reported haul ducation. HaulDuration: Type data collected, e.g., raised 1 hour raw.

title: Get numbers and weights summary of species by haul — dr_con_by_haul
text: final output summary haul identifiers species, enabling overview catch numbers weights. Additionally, function aims provide similar weight calculations icesDatras::getCatchWgt, making results comparable external references.

title: Get numbers and weights summary of species by haul — dr_con_by_haul
text: ".id" variables Survey, Year, Quarter, Country, Platform, Gear, StationName HaulNumber catenated, separated ":".

what: Connect to Raw ICES DATRAS Tables — dr_con_raw
title: Connect to Raw ICES DATRAS Tables — dr_con_raw
text: Opens lazy DuckDB connection "-" DATRAS parquet files — raw tables downloaded ICES datacenter, original old-style column names (e.g. Ship, HaulNo, ShootLat). Use need unmodified ICES output rather tidied versions provided dr_con().

title: Connect to Raw ICES DATRAS Tables — dr_con_raw
code: dr_con_raw(table = "HH")

title: Connect to Raw ICES DATRAS Tables — dr_con_raw
text: table character string specifying table. One "HH" (default), "HL", "CA", "FL", "LT", "CPUEL", "CPUEA", "CW", "IDX".

title: Connect to Raw ICES DATRAS Tables — dr_con_raw
text: lazy duckdbfs tibble. Pipe dplyr verbs call dplyr::collect() bring data memory.

title: Connect to Raw ICES DATRAS Tables — dr_con_raw
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   dr_con_raw("FL") |> dplyr::glimpse()    dr_con_raw("FL") |>     dplyr::filter(Survey == "NS-IBTS", Year == 2020) |>     dplyr::collect() } # }

title: Download DATRAS parquet files — dr_download
text: Downloads HH, HL, CA parquet files

title: Download DATRAS parquet files — dr_download
code: dr_download(   recordtype = c("HH", "HL", "CA"),   url = "https://heima.hafro.is/~einarhj/datras",   dest_directory = "data" )

title: Download DATRAS parquet files — dr_download
text: recordtype Character. Base directory parquet files written. url path, default "https://heima.hafro./~einarhj/datras" dest_directory path local storage directory (default "data")

title: Download DATRAS parquet files — dr_download
text: Invisibly returns NULL. Called side effects.

what: Download and Import DATRAS Data — dr_get
title: Download and Import DATRAS Data — dr_get
text: Retrieves DATRAS trawl survey data various sources: "parquet": Reads full dataset URL-hosted Parquet files (survey/year/quarter filtering). "old": Retrieves data via legacy icesDatras::getDATRAS function. "new": Retrieves data via icesDatras::get_datras_unaggregated_data.

title: Download and Import DATRAS Data — dr_get
code: dr_get(   recordtype,   surveys = NULL,   years = 1965:2030,   quarters = 1:4,   aphia = NULL,   from = "parquet",   quiet = TRUE )

title: Download and Import DATRAS Data — dr_get
text: recordtype string specifying record type: "HH", "HL", "CA", "FL" (flex file), "LT" (litter assessment), "CPUEL" (CPUE per length per haul per hour), "CPUEA" (CPUE per age per haul per hour), "CW" (catch weight species haul), "IDX" (age-based survey indices). surveys character vector survey IDs. NULL (default), ICES surveys excluding test surveys used (via icesDatras::getSurveyList()). years integer vector years (e.g. 1965:2030). quarters integer vector quarters (e.g. 1:4). aphia integer vector WoRMS Aphia species codes. Used "CW" "IDX". NULL, defaults cod (126436), haddock (126437), herring (126417). String specifying data source HH/HL/CA: "parquet" (default), "old", "new". Ignored recordtype = "FL". quiet Logical; suppresses progress messages TRUE (default).

title: Download and Import DATRAS Data — dr_get
text: data frame.

title: Download and Import DATRAS Data — dr_get
text: recordtype = "FL" (flex file), icesDatras::getFlexFile called every combination survey, year, quarter; argument ignored.

title: Download and Import DATRAS Data — dr_get
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   dr_get("HH")                                                      # full parquet   dr_get("HH", surveys = "NS-IBTS", years = 2020:2023, from = "new")   dr_get("FL", surveys = "NS-IBTS", years = 2020:2023, quarters = 1) } # }

what: DATRAS field type lookup table — dr_lookup_fields
title: DATRAS field type lookup table — dr_lookup_fields
text: table column names data types across DATRAS record types. Used internally .dr_settypes() coerce columns consistent types fetching data. Regenerate data-raw/DATASET_lookup_fields.R.

title: DATRAS field type lookup table — dr_lookup_fields
code: dr_lookup_fields

what: Translate column names of a data.frame or lazy tibble using a dictionary — dr_translate
title: Translate column names of a data.frame or lazy tibble using a dictionary — dr_translate
text: function allows renaming column names input object (data.frame tbl_lazy) translating user-supplied dictionary. Users can choose translate column names "old" "new" vice versa.

title: Translate column names of a data.frame or lazy tibble using a dictionary — dr_translate
code: dr_translate(d, dictionary, from = "old", to = "new")

title: Translate column names of a data.frame or lazy tibble using a dictionary — dr_translate
text: d data.frame tbl_lazy object whose column names need translated. dictionary data.frame (tibble) least two columns: (default names "old" "new"). column contains current column names d, column contains new column names translate . string specifying column name dictionary use current column name matching. Defaults "old". string specifying column name dictionary new column names translate . Defaults "new".

title: Translate column names of a data.frame or lazy tibble using a dictionary — dr_translate
text: object class d column names translated.

title: Translate column names of a data.frame or lazy tibble using a dictionary — dr_translate
code: # Example dictionary dictionary <- data.frame(     old = c("Ship", "SweepLngt"),     new = c("Platform", "SweepLength") ) # Example data df <- data.frame(Ship = "26D4", SweepLngt = 110)  dr_translate(df, dictionary) #>   Platform SweepLength #> 1     26D4         110

what: Download unaggregated DATRAS survey data — get_datras_unaggregated_data

text: Downloads unaggregated haul- biological-level data ICES DATRAS Download API. #' @author Vaishav Soni, International Council Exploration Sea (ICES)

title: Download unaggregated DATRAS survey data — get_datras_unaggregated_data
code: get_datras_unaggregated_data(recordtype, survey, year, quarter)

title: Download unaggregated DATRAS survey data — get_datras_unaggregated_data
text: recordtype Character. One "HH", "HL", "CA". survey Character. Survey acronym (e.g. "NS-IBTS"). year Character. Year range (e.g. "2020" "1965:2025"). quarter Character. Quarter range (e.g. "1" "1:4").

title: Download unaggregated DATRAS survey data — get_datras_unaggregated_data
text: data.table containing requested DATRAS data.

title: Download unaggregated DATRAS survey data — get_datras_unaggregated_data
text: function downloads zipped CSV file official ICES DATRAS API, extracts locally, reads using fixed column classes avoid costly type guessing.

title: Download unaggregated DATRAS survey data — get_datras_unaggregated_data
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ df <- get_datras_unaggregated_data(   recordtype = "HH",   survey = "NS-IBTS",   year = "1965:2025",   quarter = "1:4" ) } # }
